steps: $K3$ is a simply-connected compact complex surface; the Euler characteristic is $\chi(K3) = 24$ (a consequence of $c_2(K3) = 24$, i.e.\ the second Chern class integrates to 24).
Since $\pi_1(K3) = 1$, $H_1 = 0$. By UCT: $H_2 = \mathbb{Z}^{22}$ (no torsion, since $H_1 = 0$ contributes no Ext).
By Poincaré duality: $H_k(K3) \cong H^{4-k}(K3) \cong H_{4-k}(K3)$. So $H_3 \cong H_1 = 0$.
The intersection form on $H_2(K3;\mathbb{Z}) \cong \mathbb{Z}^{22}$ is $E_8 \oplus E_8 \oplus H \oplus H \oplus H$ (three hyperbolic planes and two $E_8$ lattices). This is a deep result.
